$comment: Single source of truth for how project-owned entities are handled when a project's resources change owner (user deletion with transfer, LDAP reset). Read by: OwnershipTransferService consumers via ownership-transfer.manifest.ts, the manifest guard test, and the n8n-local-rules/project-owned-entity-transfer lint rule via the eslint configs of @n8n/db and cli. Adding a new project-owned entity requires an entry here: either in 'transferred' (and handling in OwnershipTransferService.transferAllResources) or in 'notTransferred' with the reason for the decision. The 'path' pins the entry to the declaring file: the lint rule rejects a same-named entity in a different file, and the guard test fails when a listed file no longer declares the entity.

notTransferred.name: ProjectRelation
notTransferred.reason: Membership rows; they describe the deleted project itself.

notTransferred.name: ProjectSecretsProviderAccess
notTransferred.reason: Per-project secrets-provider config; scoped to the deleted project.

notTransferred.name: RoleMappingRule
notTransferred.reason: SSO role-mapping config attached to the project, not user data.

notTransferred.name: Variables
notTransferred.reason: Project-scoped variables are dropped via FK cascade; personal projects do not expose them.

notTransferred.name: Agent
notTransferred.reason: Dropped via FK cascade today. Revisit: agents created by the deleted user are lost on transfer.

notTransferred.name: AgentExecutionThread
notTransferred.reason: Dropped via FK cascade together with its Agent.

notTransferred.name: InstanceAiThread
notTransferred.reason: AI assistant conversation history; personal to the deleted user.

notTransferred.name: InsightsMetadata
notTransferred.reason: Denormalized analytics snapshot; historical insights keep the original project reference by design.
